title: Getting Started with RestoreAssist — Create Your First Inspection, Moisture Map & Scope in Minutes
description: A step-by-step walkthrough of RestoreAssist for Australian restoration technicians — creating your first inspection, building a moisture map, running the equipment calculator, and generating a scope narrative. From sign-up to insurance-ready report.
author: Phill McGurk | RestoreAssist
transcript:
## Welcome to RestoreAssist

RestoreAssist is a purpose-built inspection and documentation platform for Australian water damage restoration technicians. It combines moisture mapping, equipment calculation, scope generation, and insurance reporting into a single workflow — replacing the disconnected combination of spreadsheets, photo apps, and word processor templates that most restoration companies currently use.

This guide walks through the core workflow from creating your first inspection to generating an insurance-ready report.

## Step 1: Create a New Inspection

From the dashboard, select 'New Inspection'. Enter the site address — RestoreAssist includes Australian address autocomplete powered by OpenStreetMap, which will populate the suburb, state, and postcode automatically.

Complete the job header:
- **Water Category**: Select Category 1, 2, or 3 based on the contamination level of the water source
- **Damage Class**: Select Class 1, 2, 3, or 4 based on the extent of material absorption
- **Loss Date**: The date and time of the loss event (used to calculate elapsed time for category escalation)
- **Attending Technician**: Your name and IICRC certification number

The Category and Class selections drive subsequent calculations throughout the inspection — equipment ratios, material dry standards, and scope line items are all generated from these two inputs.

## Step 2: Build the Moisture Map

The moisture mapping canvas is an SVG-based floor plan tool. For a new property, you can sketch the floor plan using the room drawing tool, or upload an existing floor plan as a background underlay.

For each affected room:
1. Place moisture reading markers at each grid point (floor, wall heights 0–100 cm, control areas)
2. Enter the moisture reading value for each marker
3. RestoreAssist automatically colour-codes readings against the IICRC S500 Appendix C threshold for the material type assigned to that point (red for wet, amber for transition zone, green for dry)

The moisture map updates in real time as you enter readings, giving you an immediate visual representation of the affected area.

## Step 3: Run the Equipment Calculator

With the damage class and affected area established from the moisture map, navigate to the Equipment Calculator. The calculator uses IICRC S500:2025 Appendix B ratios to calculate:

- Number of air movers required
- Dehumidifier capacity required (L/day) and recommended unit quantity
- Air scrubber requirement (based on category and affected volume)
- AS/NZS 3012:2019 electrical load check (80% circuit capacity rule)

Equipment items are added to the scope automatically, with Appendix B references embedded in the line item justification.

## Step 4: Generate the Scope Narrative

The scope generation tool uses the inspection data — category, class, affected materials, moisture readings, and equipment selections — to produce a structured scope narrative. The narrative is generated using the Claude AI system and includes:

- Executive summary of the loss event and assessment findings
- IICRC S500:2025 category and class determination with basis
- Affected area description with moisture map reference
- Proposed methodology for each affected material
- Equipment specification with IICRC Appendix B justification
- Drying goal and monitoring plan
- Estimated drying duration

The narrative is editable before export, allowing the technician to add site-specific observations or revise any section.

## Step 5: Export the Insurance Report

The final report package includes the scope narrative, moisture map, photo documentation, equipment list, and the company header. It exports to PDF in a format designed for direct submission to insurance adjusters.

All documentation is stored in the RestoreAssist cloud, accessible from any device, and linked to the inspection record for future daily monitoring updates.
videoScript:
G'day, welcome to RestoreAssist. I'm Phill McGurk, and in this video I want to walk you through creating your first inspection on the platform — from opening the app to having an insurance-ready report in hand.

The whole idea behind RestoreAssist is to replace the disconnected tools most of us use: a spreadsheet for readings, a different app for photos, a Word document for the scope. RestoreAssist pulls all of that into one workflow so you're not reassembling documents at the end of every job.

Let's start from the beginning.

From the dashboard, hit 'New Inspection'. Enter the site address — there's an Australian address autocomplete that fills in the suburb, state, and postcode for you.

Then complete the job header. Two fields are critical: water category and damage class. These two selections drive every calculation and scope recommendation in the entire inspection. Select Category 1, 2, or 3 for contamination level, and Class 1 through 4 for the extent of material absorption. Also enter the loss date and time — RestoreAssist uses the time gap between loss and inspection to flag potential category escalation.

Next, the moisture map. The canvas is a floor plan tool where you drop markers at each moisture reading location. Enter the reading value at each point, and the system automatically colour-codes it against the IICRC S500 Appendix C threshold for the material type you've assigned — red for wet, amber for transition zone, green for confirmed dry. You can sketch the floor plan directly in the tool or upload an existing plan as a background underlay.

Once you've got your affected area established from the moisture map, go to the equipment calculator. Enter the damage class and the affected area, and it calculates your air mover quantity, dehumidifier capacity, air scrubber requirement, and runs the AS/NZS 3012 80% electrical load check. The equipment items go straight into the scope with Appendix B references in the justification.

For the scope narrative, the generation tool uses all your inspection data — category, class, affected materials, readings, equipment — and produces a full structured scope. It's AI-assisted, IICRC-cited, and editable before export.

The final report exports to PDF with the moisture map, scope narrative, photo documentation, and equipment list — ready for the adjuster.

Everything is stored in the cloud and accessible from any device for your daily monitoring visits.

Head to restoreassist.app to start your free trial — your first inspection is on us.
